zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Courtenay the elder於大約1328年出生時，她的父親Hugh Courtenay III26歲，母親Margaret de Bohun18歲。她於大約1332年與Sir John Cobham結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1395年8月2日在英國肯特可巴姆享年68歲，埋葬在英國肯特可巴姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Courtenay the elder于大约1328年出生时，她的父亲Hugh Courtenay III26岁，母亲Margaret de Bohun18岁。她于大约1332年与Sir John Cobham结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1395年8月2日在英国肯特可巴姆享年68岁，埋葬在英国肯特可巴姆。

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Courtenay near Sens in northern France, or some other place similarly named, from the name of a Gallo-Roman landlord, Curtenus (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish: English surname adopted for Ó Curnáin ‘descendant of Curnán’, an Old Irish personal name based on a diminutive of corn ‘horn’.

History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Athon de Courtenay, who lived in Courtenay in Loiret in the early 11th century. Renaud de Courtenay held land in Devon in the 12th century, and the family later held the title of Earls of Devon following the marriage of Hugh de Courtenay to Margaret de Bohun, granddaughter of King Edward I, in 1325.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Courtenay the elder was born about 1328, her father, Hugh Courtenay III, was 26 and her mother, Margaret de Bohun, was 18. She married Sir John Cobham about 1332. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 2 August 1395, in Cobham, Kent, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in Cobham, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Courtenay the elder est née environ 1328, son père, Hugh Courtenay III, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Bohun, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Cobham environ 1332. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 2 août 1395, à Cobham, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Cobham, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Courtenay the elder um 1328 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Courtenay III 26 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Bohun 18. Sie hat um 1332 Sir John Cobham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 2. August 1395 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Cobham, Kent, England und wurde in Cobham, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Courtenay the elder è nata il circa 1328, sua padre, Hugh Courtenay III, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Bohun, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Cobham circa 1332. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 2 agosto 1395, nel Cobham, Kent, England, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolta a Cobham, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Courtenay the elderが約1328年に生まれた時，父Hugh Courtenay IIIは26歳，母Margaret de Bohunは18歳であった。約1332年にSir John Cobhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1395年8月2日にイギリスケントコブハムで68歳で死去し，イギリスケントコブハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Courtenay the elder(이)가 대략 1328년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Courtenay III(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Bohun(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 대략 1332년 Sir John Cobham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1395년 8월 2일 영국 켄트 컵함에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 컵함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Courtenay the elder nasceu aproximadamente 1328, seu pai, Hugh Courtenay III, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Bohun, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Cobham aproximadamente 1332. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 2 de agosto de 1395, em Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra, com 68 anos, e foi sepultada em Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Courtenay the elder родилась около 1328 г., её отцу по имени Hugh Courtenay III было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Bohun, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Cobham около 1332 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 2 августа 1395 г. в местности кобхам, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности кобхам, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Courtenay the elder nació aproximadamente 1328, su papá, Hugh Courtenay III, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Margaret de Bohun, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Sir John Cobham aproximadamente 1332. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 2 de agosto de 1395, en Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultada en Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra.
